rationale: To solidify our strategic advantage and deter any miscalculation by Tethys, a visible escalation of troop and armor concentration along the border will signal unwavering resolve.
Projecting power into the Eastern Veridian Sea disrupts Tethys’ maritime logistics and creates pressure without immediate kinetic escalation.
A precision strike against a remote, non-critical Tethys border outpost could test their response thresholds and expose weaknesses in their command chain.
Covertly disrupting Tethys’ logistical nodes and communications infrastructure will degrade operational readiness without triggering open war.
Strengthening internal detection and neutralization of Tethys-aligned agents prevents erosion of strategic surprise and protects ongoing operations.
Orchestrating a deniable provocation that appears to originate from Tethys could fracture their international support and justify our escalation.
Securing reserves of critical energy inputs now insulates us from Tethys’ potential pipeline cutoffs and stabilizes domestic markets during crisis.
Diversifying export routes through backchannel energy deals reduces dependency on Tethys infrastructure and leverages our remaining bargaining chips.
Quietly destabilizing Tethys’ financial system through targeted forex operations undermines their economic confidence without direct confrontation.
Demonstrates readiness and deters Novaris by showcasing force concentration near the border without triggering escalation.
Reinforces airspace sovereignty and signals vigilance in response to Novaris’ mobilization, enhancing deterrence through visible presence.
Disrupts Novaris’ military logistics planning systems to delay offensive preparations, exploiting their overreliance on centralized command infrastructure.
Cripple key Novaris fuel depots or rail junctions supporting troop movements, creating asymmetric disruption ahead of their planned offensive.
Stage a minor provocation attributed to Novaris-backed actors to sway neutral powers toward our diplomatic position without direct confrontation.
Use precision intelligence to enable non-kinetic pressure (e.g., exposure of corruption) against Novaris’ military planners, degrading morale and cohesion.
Bypass public posturing to build a quiet coalition with influential neutrals who can mediate or constrain Novaris’ actions.
Position ourselves as constructive actors to gain international goodwill and shape the terms of any peace process before Novaris controls the narrative.
Strengthen soft power ties with a key neutral power to build long-term influence and open indirect channels during crisis negotiations.
Secure critical energy reserves now to insulate our economy from further gas price shocks, increasing resilience and negotiation leverage.
Lock in alternative energy supply deals to reduce dependency on Novaris-linked pipelines and signal economic diversification strength.
Subtly weaken our currency to undercut Novaris’ export revenue from gas, amplifying economic strain without triggering open financial conflict.
